results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THE PROJECT IS TO ASSESS PRACTICAL AND EFFECTIVE APPROACHES TO REDUCE INDOOR EXPOSURE TO PARTICULATE MATTER (PM) OF OUTDOOR WILDLAND FIRE ORIGIN IN SINGLE-FAMILY HOUSING IN THE WESTERN U.S. THE PROJECT WILL CONDUCT FIELD STUDIES TO ASSESS A VARIETY OF SUSTAINABLE AND PRACTICAL INTERVENTIONS FOR REDUCING INDOOR PM LEVELS, SUCH AS INDOOR PARTICLE FILTRATION, VENTILATION STRATEGIES, AND BUILDING SEALING STRATEGIES. IN ADDITION, MODEL SIMULATIONS WILL BE USED TO INFER THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE INTERVENTIONS FOR VARIOUS SINGLE-FAMILY HOMES TYPES FOUND IN THE WESTERN US.ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL CONDUCT FIELD STUDIES TO ASSESS A VARIETY OF SUSTAINABLE AND PRACTICAL INTERVENTIONS FOR REDUCING INDOOR PM LEVELS, SUCH AS INDOOR PARTICLE FILTRATION, VENTILATION STRATEGIES, AND BUILDING SEALING STRATEGIES. IN ADDITION, MODEL SIMULATIONS WILL BE USED TO INFER THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE INTERVENTIONS FOR VARIOUS SINGLE-FAMILY HOMES TYPES FOUND IN THE WESTERN US.SUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES:THE PROJECT WILL CONDUCT FIELD STUDIES TO ASSESS A VARIETY OF SUSTAINABLE AND PRACTICAL INTERVENTIONS FOR REDUCING INDOOR PM LEVELS, SUCH AS INDOOR PARTICLE FILTRATION, VENTILATION STRATEGIES, AND BUILDING SEALING STRATEGIES. IN ADDITION, MODEL SIMULATIONS WILL BE USED TO INFER THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE INTERVENTIONS FOR VARIOUS SINGLE-FAMILY HOMES TYPES FOUND IN THE WESTERN US.

DESCRIPTION:
THE GOAL OF THE PROJECT IS TO ASSESS PRACTICAL AND EFFECTIVE APPROACHES TO REDUCE INDOOR EXPOSURE TO PARTICULATE MATTER (PM) OF OUTDOOR WILDLAND FIRE ORIGIN IN SINGLE-FAMILY HOUSING IN THE WESTERN U.S. THE PROJECT WILL CONDUCT FIELD STUDIES TO ASSESS A VARIETY OF SUSTAINABLE AND PRACTICAL INTERVENTIONS FOR REDUCING INDOOR PM LEVELS, SUCH AS INDOOR PARTICLE FILTRATION, VENTILATION STRATEGIES, AND BUILDING SEALING STRATEGIES. IN ADDITION, MODEL SIMULATIONS WILL BE USED TO INFER THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE INTERVENTIONS FOR VARIOUS SINGLE-FAMILY HOMES TYPES FOUND IN THE WESTERN US.
